topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the ballroom shimmered with the warmth of celebration, a symphony of laughter and string music washing over me. I was mid-conversation with Sarah, recounting a ridiculous anecdote about a past vacation, when a familiar silhouette caught my eye across the crowded floor. Time seemed to slow. It was him. My ex. He was standing near the refreshment table, looking impossibly sharp in his tuxedo, and the moment our eyes met, a jolt went through me that made my breath hitch. I immediately straightened my posture, a wide, involuntary smile spreading across my face, and I excused myself from Sarah, making a beeline toward him. When we finally managed to speak, the easy familiarity of our past clicked back into place, and I found myself laughing, a sound that felt bright and unrestrained.

[story 2]

I was navigating the throng of well-wishers, trying to keep up with the flow of the evening, when I spotted him near the entrance, holding a glass of champagne. My internal monologue stuttered for a fraction of a second before I caught sight of him. A genuine, buoyant feeling bubbled up inside my chest, making my shoulders relax and my gaze linger. I took a deep breath, smoothed down my dress, and started moving across the room with a purpose that felt entirely new. As I approached, he turned, and the recognition in his eyes sparked something immediate and electric. I offered him a warm, expansive smile, letting my eyes convey everything I felt—a sheer, unadulterated joy that made my whole frame feel lighter.

[story 3]

The noise of the wedding reception was a comforting backdrop, but then I saw him. He was leaning against a pillar, talking animatedly with a group of old acquaintances, and suddenly the entire room seemed to focus on the space between us. A warmth flooded my extremities, and I felt an undeniable urge to walk over, to bridge the distance. I stopped talking mid-sentence, a small, pleased expression playing on my lips. When he finally noticed me, he paused his conversation, his expression shifting into one of genuine surprise, and I felt a rush of pure satisfaction. I walked toward him, my steps light and deliberate, ready to reconnect in the midst of the festivities.
